description:
Extend call-site observation (fz-ul4.4.1) to higher-order callees.

Problem: fz-ul4.4.1 only accumulates obs when callee is Expr::Var(name) with name in user_fns. For HOF use like

  fn map(_, []), do: []
  fn map(f, [h | t]), do: [f(h) | map(f, t)]
  fn double(x), do: x * 2
  map(double, [1, 2, 3])

the f(h) call inside map's body has callee Var("f") which isn't a user_fn, so we don't accumulate obs and f's type stays Pattern::Var(any). Result: map types as (any, any) → any and falls out of JIT/AOT eligibility — even though the program is fully monomorphic in practice.

Approach: track 'fn-valued parameter origin' per call site. When a fn parameter is bound at call sites consistently to a single user fn (or a set we can join), substitute that fn's arrow type in place of  for the parameter binding. Specifically:

1. At every call site of a user fn f, record the *types* of arg positions that hold fn values (per-arrow types, not just any). 2. In type_function, after pattern_bindings on a fn-typed param, replace its env binding with the union of observed fn types for that param position. 3. Inside the body, calls to that param now apply_arrow against a real arrow rather than 'any'.

This is interim coverage before fz-ul4.6 (parametric polymorphism) — call-site monomorphization gives us most of the benefit for closed programs without needing full generalization.

Concrete acceptance: `fn map(f, [h | t]), do: [f(h) | map(f, t)]` typed precisely when called with double, drives a JIT-eligible map. Add an end-to-end test in jit::tests.

Risk: when a fn-param is observed bound to multiple incompatible fns, we degrade to union-of-arrows; apply_arrow already handles that path.
title: Typer: higher-order call-site observation for fn-valued parameters
